response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 performs remediation in an event‑driven way: compliance findings are published to a central Event Grid topic (azr-fleet-platform-events) and an automation subscriber (automation_policies_topic_trigger) listens to that topic and kicks off the remediation workflows.

Key points from the architecture we have:
- Events for remediation are emitted on the azr-fleet-platform-events Event Grid topic.
- automation_policies_topic_trigger is the subscriber that depends on that topic and is responsible for launching the automation/policy remediation actions.
- This decouples detection from remediation (multiple consumers can subscribe to the same topic), and ties remediation into the Fleet Platform event stream so fleet services can integrate or observe actions.

If you need the exact runbook/automation implementation details (what playbooks/runbooks are executed, auth used, retry/backoff, where status is recorded), that level of detail isn’t present in the provided context.

Root causes: /api/aks was classifying runners from the ACI containerGroups list payload, which often omitted full instanceView/currentState data, so activeRunner went null after refresh; and runner logs from ACI came back as timestamp-prefixed JSON lines, so the browser formatter treated them as raw text. Fixes: in failure-lab-portal/api/app.py, list_aks_ru", "2026-04-13: On 2026-04-13 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Wisdm-POC/azr-failure-labs-demo while patching the AKS operator page, a live deployment issue and a UI correctness fix were confirmed. The failure-lab portal deploy script depends on the Static Web Apps CLI, which now requires Node 18+; when the shell PATH resolved to Node 16.15.1, the API zip deploy succeeded but the Static Web App front-end publish failed, leaving old aks.html/aks.js live until redeployed with PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.20.0/bi", "2026-04-13: Generated a fleet-wide prod report for Agent Fleet Platform covering 2026-04-11 14:28:59 UTC to 2026-04-13 14:28:59 UTC. DB-backed findings: 20,317 eventgrid_resource_events across 230 subscriptions; 713 actionable, 19,604 non-actionable, 0 redeliveries. Processing status: 19,924 captured_no_action, 393 completed. Top event skip reasons: unsupported_resource_type_write 13,373; delete_event_not_actioned 5,398; vm_os_unresolved 523; resource_action_cooldown_active 320; service_block_mismatch 286; ", "2026-04-10: On 2026-04-10 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Wisdm-POC, fixed the dedicated AKS operator page data path after live rollout. Root causes were all in failure-lab-portal/api/app.py: the /api/aks cluster-state command used brittle kubectl jsonpath expressions that failed under AKS Run Command shell semantics, latest_aks_runner preferred an old manual container group by lexicographic name sorting, and run_aks_command treated AKS managedClusters/runCommand as a synchronous ARM call instead of a long-run", "2026-04-10: On 2026-04-10 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Wisdm-POC while deploying/testing the AKS failure lab in wisdm-poc-platform, several live issues were found and fixed. The demo-api baseline was unstable because the inline Python server in aks-failure-lab/manifests/demo-api.yaml used HTTPServer and did not handle BrokenPipeError under kubelet probes; switching to ThreadingHTTPServer, explicit /healthz handling, HTTP/1.0, Connection: close, and swallowed BrokenPipeError/ConnectionResetError restored a h"]}
